zh.Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Deuts: 德國貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫
zh.Gangloff, Pfalz, Bayern, Deutschland: 德國貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫
zh.lifeSummary: Anna Elisabetha Nageln是Joh. Nickel Nagel的女兒，於1730年出生。她於1772年與Johann Phillip Nagel在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和10個女兒的父母。她去世於1778年12月7日享年48歲，埋葬在德國貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫。

zh-hans.Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Deuts: 德国貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫
zh-hans.Gangloff, Pfalz, Bayern, Deutschland: 德国貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Anna Elisabetha Nageln是Joh. Nickel Nagel的女儿，于1730年出生。她于1772年与Johann Phillip Nagel在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和10个女儿的父母。她去世于1778年12月7日享年48岁，埋葬在德国貝也藍德可萊斯 庫瑟爾岡格洛夫。

nameMeanings.name: Nageln
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Jutta, Erwin, Rainer, Siegfried.
South German (Nägele): variant of Nagel . Compare Naegele .

variants.name: Nagel
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Heinz, Hans, Otto, Kurt, Armin, Franz, Fritz, Udo, Wilhelm, Aloys.
German, Dutch, Scandinavian, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): from Middle High German, Middle Dutch nagel, German Nagel ‘nail’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of nails. Compare Nail .
Germanized form of Sorbian Nahł, Nagł, Nahły, and Nagły: nickname from Upper Sorbian nahły, Lower Sorbian nagły ‘quick’, also ‘hot-tempered’.

nameMeanings.name: Anna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

variants.name: Anne
meanings: variant of Anne , in common use as a given name in most European languages. Among people with a classical education, it has from time to time been associated with Virgil's Aeneid, where it is borne by the sister of Dido , Queen of Carthage. This Phoenician name may ultimately be of Semitic origin, and thus related to the biblical Anne. However, the connection, if it exists, is indirect rather than direct.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabetha
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

en.lifeSummary: Anna Elisabetha Nageln was born in 1730 as the daughter of Joh. Nickel Nagel. She married Johann Phillip Nagel in 1772, in Gangloff, Kusel, Bavaria, Germany. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 10 daughters. She died on 7 December 1778, at the age of 48, and was buried in Gangloff, Kusel, Bavaria, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Anna Elisabetha Nageln est née en 1730, fille de Joh. Nickel Nagel. Elle a épousé Johann Phillip Nagel en 1772, à Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 10 filles. Elle est décédée le 7 décembre 1778, à l’âge de 48 ans, et a été enterrée à Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Anna Elisabetha Nageln wurde 1730, als Tochter von Joh. Nickel Nagel geboren. Sie hat 1772 in Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Deutschland Johann Phillip Nagel geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 10 Töchter. Sie starb am 7. Dezember 1778 im Alter von 48 Jahren und wurde in Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Anna Elisabetha Nageln è nata il nel 1730 come figlia di Joh. Nickel Nagel. Lei sposato Johann Phillip Nagel nel 1772, nel Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 10 figlie. Morì il 7 dicembre 1778, all'età di 48 anni, e fu sepolta a Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Germania.

ja.Evangelisch-Reformierte Kirche, Gangloff: ドイツバイエルンプファルズ
ja.Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Deuts: ドイツバイエルンランドクレイス クセルガングロッフ
ja.Gangloff, Pfalz, Bayern, Deutschland: ドイツバイエルンランドクレイス クセルガングロッフ
ja.lifeSummary: Anna Elisabetha Nagelnは1730年にJoh. Nickel Nagelの娘として生まれた。1772年にドイツバイエルンランドクレイス クセルガングロッフでJohann Phillip Nagelと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と10人の娘の親であった。彼女は1778年12月7日に48歳で死去し，ドイツバイエルンランドクレイス クセルガングロッフにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Anna Elisabetha Nageln(은)는 1730년 Joh. Nickel Nagel의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 1772년 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 쿠셀 갱글로프에서 Johann Phillip Nagel(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 열 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1778년 12월 7일 48세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 베이른 랜드크레이스 쿠셀 갱글로프에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Anna Elisabetha Nageln nasceu em 1730 filha de Joh. Nickel Nagel. Ela casou-se com Johann Phillip Nagel em 1772, em Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 10 filhas. Ela faleceu em 7 de dezembro de 1778, com 48 anos, e foi sepultada em Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Anna Elisabetha Nageln родилась в 1730 г. Её отца звали Joh. Nickel Nagel. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Johann Phillip Nagel в 1772 г. в местности ганглофф, ландкрис кусел, беиирн, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 10 дочерей. Она умерла 7 декабря 1778 г. в возрасте 48 лет и была похоронена в местности ганглофф, ландкрис кусел, беиирн, Германия.

es.lifeSummary: Anna Elisabetha Nageln nació en 1730 como hija de Joh. Nickel Nagel. Se casó con Johann Phillip Nagel en 1772, en Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 10 hijas. Murió el 7 de diciembre de 1778, a la edad de 48 años, y fue sepultada en Gangloff, Landkreis Kusel, Bayern, Alemania.
